skill.name: readability
skill.description: Analyze text readability using established formulas and return scores, audience guidance, and practical writing recommendations.
skill.summary: Measure readability scores and identify practical ways to make text easier to understand.
skill.author: humanizerai

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown content, readability formulas, and output placeholders. No executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt injection instructions were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Detected as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command finding at SKILL.md:40 points to a Markdown code fence that begins an example output format. It does not execute shell, Ruby, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is only a Markdown delimiter for a sample report. There is no executable code or command invocation around it.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Readability Text Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:14 refer to description and input text for a readability analyzer. No hash algorithm, encryption call, or credential processing is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are natural-language skill metadata and input instructions. They contain no cryptographic implementation or security-sensitive transformation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Formula Placeholders Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings at SKILL.md:23 and SKILL.md:47 point to readability grade formulas and output placeholders. They do not request host, user, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is limited to readability scoring labels. No semantic evidence suggests system discovery or local machine inspection.

content.user_title: Analyze Readability and Improve Plain Language
content.value_statement: Complex text can block readers from understanding important information. This skill scores readability and gives targeted edits for clearer writing.
actual_capabilities: Calculates Flesch Reading Ease and explains the score.
Estimates Flesch-Kincaid, Gunning Fog, and SMOG grade levels.
Reports word count, sentence count, sentence length, and complex word rate.
Identifies likely audience fit based on readability ranges.
Suggests specific ways to shorten sentences and simplify wording.
limitations: Scores are estimates and depend on syllable counting accuracy.
Passive voice detection is approximate and may miss edge cases.
It does not rewrite the full text unless the user asks.
It is not a substitute for audience testing or legal review.

use_cases.title: Review Customer Help Content
use_cases.description: Check support articles for sentence length, grade level, and plain-language improvement opportunities.

use_cases.title: Prepare Public Communications
use_cases.description: Evaluate announcements, notices, and emails before publishing them to a broad audience.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Documentation
use_cases.description: Find dense passages in guides and convert the recommendations into clearer documentation tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Readability Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a quick score and audience estimate.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the readability of this text and explain the main scores in simple language: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Plain-Language Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the text should work for a general audience.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this text for readability. List the top three changes that would make it easier for a general audience: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Readability Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when reviewing help docs, procedures, or guides.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this documentation for readability. Include score estimates, difficult sections, and specific edit recommendations: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Specific Optimization
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a target reading level or audience.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this text against a target of [target audience or grade level]. Explain gaps and recommend edits to reach that target: [paste text]

output_examples.input: A short policy notice with long sentences and several technical terms.
output_examples.output: The analysis reports a difficult reading level, highlights long average sentence length, and recommends shorter sentences with simpler terms.

output_examples.input: A customer help article for setting up an account.
output_examples.output: The result shows a standard reading level, identifies a few complex words, and suggests clearer steps for first-time users.

output_examples.input: A technical guide intended for experienced developers.
output_examples.output: The report explains that higher grade-level scores may be acceptable, while still flagging sentences that slow scanning.

best_practices: Provide the full passage so sentence and word counts are meaningful.
State the target audience or grade level when you know it.
Use the recommendations as editing guidance, then review the revised text again.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one score as the only measure of quality.
Do not use it to judge text in languages it was not designed for.
Do not ignore domain terms that readers must know.

faq.question: What readability metrics does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses Flesch Reading Ease, Flesch-Kincaid Grade, Gunning Fog, SMOG, and supporting text statistics.

faq.question: Can it rewrite my text?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on analysis and recommendations. You can ask for a rewrite after reviewing the findings.

faq.question: Is the grade level exact?
faq.answer: No. Grade levels are estimates based on formulas, sentence length, and word complexity.

faq.question: Does it work for technical writing?
faq.answer: Yes, but required technical terms may raise scores even when they are appropriate for the audience.

faq.question: Can it detect passive voice?
faq.answer: It can estimate passive voice sentences, but grammar edge cases may require human review.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
